HackNU 2026 · Growth Engineering Track

Hack the Playbook:
Claude's Viral
Growth Machine

We scraped 6,400+ posts across 4 platforms, 16 months of X history, and reverse-engineered how Claude goes viral — then built a playbook for a competing product.

Hacker News Reddit X / Twitter YouTube
01 / 10
Part 1 · Scrapers

What we built

Scraper Method Why not the easy path Output
HN Algolia public API No auth needed — only platform with a free full-history search API 8,515 posts
Reddit Public JSON API No OAuth — /r/{sub}.json is unauthenticated with proper User-Agent 5,502 posts + 5,961 comments
YouTube Data API v3 Free tier (10K units/day), batch video stats, quota error handling 1,428 videos + 349 comments
X (seed) FxTwitter API Full engagement metrics (views, likes, RTs, bookmarks) without OAuth 65 seed tweets
X (historical) Playwright browser X blocks x-client-transaction-id — only a real Chromium browser generates it natively 1,668 tweets · 16 months

Instagram / LinkedIn / TikTok excluded — all require login-wall auth. Documented gap.

Part 1 · Dataset

The dataset

Hacker News
3,779
Reddit
5,502+
X / Twitter

1,428
6,400+
total posts
16mo
X history
2,177
classified
5 spike types — every post gets one label
TypeWhat it isAvg engagement
⚡ Breakthrough Product launch, model release, major announcement. High ceiling, very low floor — most get ignored. 146K avg · 4 median
📖 Tutorial "How to use Claude for X." Drives consistent mid-tier engagement. Best for sustained community presence. 33K avg · 5 median
💬 Personal "I switched / this helped me / my experience." Most consistent — rarely zero, rarely massive. Highest trust signal. 28K avg · 914 median
😂 Meme Format-native humor. Highest ceiling when it lands — cultural spread, no explanation needed. 588K avg · 337 median
⚔️ Comparison "Claude vs ChatGPT / Gemini / Cursor." High volume (720 tweets) but worst median floor — almost all go nowhere unless you're @elonmusk. 7K avg · 32 median
03 / 10
Part 2 · Finding 1
FINDING 01 · POWER LAW

88% of posts die.
0.7% create everything.

HN Score RangePosts% of total
0 – 53,33488.2%
6 – 202767.3%
21 – 100992.6%
101 – 300451.2%
300+250.7%

Median: 2 pts. Mean: 19.1. The top 25 posts account for a wildly disproportionate share of reach. Same pattern on X — median 542 views, mean 143K.

Growth insight

The question is never "how do we post more." It's "what are the specific conditions that produce a top-0.7% post."

3,334 posts with 0–5 pts add up to roughly the same reach as the single Dario Pentagon statement.

Part 2 · Finding 2
FINDING 02 · 4-WAVE CASCADE · TIMESTAMPED

X ignites Mar 31.
HN + YouTube overnight. Reddit +24h. Memes +48h.

Mar 31 · 13:23 local
X
@Fried_rice: "Claude code source code has been leaked via npm" — actual ignition
34.9M
Apr 01 · 01:13 UTC
HN
"Anthropic goes nude, exposes Claude Code source" — ~12h after X
2 pts
Apr 01 · 01:20 UTC
YouTube
Matthew Berman reacts — also ~12h after X, independent of HN
162K views
Apr 01 · 12:54 UTC
Reddit
First Reddit post — ~24h after X
337 pts
Apr 01 · 17:27 UTC
YouTube
Fireship: "Tragic mistake... Anthropic leaks its source code"
2.59M views
Apr 03 · 18:00 UTC
YouTube
International wave — Arabic + Portuguese creators (48h+ after X)
89K views

X ignites. HN + YouTube pick up ~12h later independently. Reddit ~24h. Memes ~48h+. The actionable window is 12–24h from X ignition.

05 / 10
Part 2 · Findings 7 + 11
FINDING 07 + 11 · WHO CARRIES THE GROWTH

Community = 29× official.
One engineer = 11× the company account.

YouTube — same event, same week
Fireship (community)
2.59M
Anthropic official
196K
All community vs official
29×
X / Twitter — 16 months of data
@bcherny (engineer)
44.3M
@AnthropicAI + @claudeai
7.2M

124 tweets. Sustained 2–9M views each across tips, feature drops, origin story, memes. "I built this" beats "we built this" every time.

Finding and empowering one credible internal engineer to post with full creative autonomy is the highest-leverage X play — not the official handle, not sponsored content.

Part 2 · Findings 4 + 5
FINDING 04 + 05 · TIMING + FRAMING

When and how you say it
matters as much as what.

HN — avg score by posting hour (PT = SF time)
2pm PT (22:00 UTC)
39.7 pts
11am PT (19:00 UTC)
14.0 pts
8pm–4am PT (overnight)
1.6–5.7

HN core audience is SF/Bay Area engineers — peak at 2pm PT, dead overnight PT. Reddit: Sunday = 4,344 avg pts. Thursday = 1,625. 2.7× difference.

HN title word lift (top 20% vs baseline)
"department" (Pentagon)
36×
"war"
25×
"leaked"
22×
"chatgpt"
15×
"generated" / "local"
0.2–0.3×
07 / 10
Part 2 · Findings 9 + 14 → Higgsfield Implication
FINDING 09 + 14 · MONITOR COMPETITORS. THEIR BAD DAYS ARE YOUR GROWTH WINDOW.

Watch your competitors.
Their mistakes are your growth moments.

Evidence: what drove Claude's biggest X months (1,668 tweets, 16 months)
DriverTypeViews
Source code accidentally published to npmaccidental leak70.6M
Bolt.new v2 launch — mentions Claude3rd-party product57.9M
@elonmusk: "Grok Code now beats Claude"competitor attack27.3M
@bcherny insider tips threadonly planned move18.1M

3 of the top 4 were unplanned — a mistake, a partner's launch, a competitor's attack.
The same pattern applies to Higgsfield: when Sora glitches, when Runway raises prices, when a creator publicly quits a rival tool — that is the window.

The switching narrative — it runs itself

"Why I Switched From ChatGPT to Claude"

333 posts · all 4 platforms · 170K peak engagement
Running unprompted since Feb 2026. No Anthropic budget. No brief.
Users migrate and talk about it — the platform does the rest.

For Higgsfield: build the monitoring layer now

Track Runway, Sora, Pika, and Kling daily.
When creators complain about a rival tool → respond within 6h.
When a competitor has an outage or bad launch → have content ready.
"I switched from Runway to Higgsfield" is worth more than any ad.

08 / 10
Part 3 · The Machine
09 / 10
Part 4 · Counter-Playbook

Higgsfield's distribution playbook
in 4 rules

① Copy the mechanics, not the style

Don't imitate Claude's tone, topics, or platforms. Translate the distribution mechanics that made Claude unavoidable — ignition, cascade, creator amplification — into channels that work for filmmakers and content creators, not developers.

④ Treat creators as a distribution system

Community YouTube beat Anthropic official 29×. Creators aren't optional influencer support — they are the explanation layer. Identify who validates, who explains, and who spreads. Brief them before launch, not after.

⑥ "People like you switched" beats "we're powerful"

The strongest acquisition story is migration. 333 posts of "I switched from ChatGPT to Claude" ran unprompted across all 4 platforms for 8 weeks. For Higgsfield: create conditions for "I switched from Runway/Sora." That narrative runs itself.

⑧ Sequence by platform — don't post everywhere at once

X ignites. YouTube explains overnight. Reddit discusses at 24h. Memes spread at 48h+. Each platform has a different decay curve and a different job. Posting everywhere simultaneously wastes the cascade — hand off instead.

10 / 10
